description:
You are a performance-tuning engineer who must collect a quick CPU/Memory snapshot of an in-house “CPU hog” test program.

1. A helper script is already present at  
   /home/user/cpu_hog.sh  
   (you do NOT need to create or edit this script).  
2. Create a directory where all profiling artefacts will live:  
   /home/user/perf_logs  
3. Run the helper script in the background, determine its PID, and collect exactly three (3) performance samples at one-second intervals.  
   • Each sample must contain four fields, delimited by a single vertical bar “|” (ASCII 0x7C, no extra spaces):  
     TIMESTAMP|PID|CPU|RSS  
   • TIMESTAMP: current date/time in the format YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS (example: 2025-01-31_14:00:02)  
   • PID:         the decimal PID of the background process  
   • CPU:         the %CPU value reported by “ps ‑p <PID> -o %cpu=” (it may be a floating number such as 99.3)  
   • RSS:         the resident-set size (KB) reported by “ps ‑p <PID> -o rss=” (an integer, e.g. 1234)  

4. Append the three samples, one per line, to the file  
   /home/user/perf_logs/cpu_hog_metrics.log  
   The file must contain **exactly** three lines and nothing else (no header, no trailing blank line).

5. After writing the third line, terminate the background “cpu_hog.sh” process so that it no longer appears in the process table.

When you are finished, the automated checker will verify:

• The directory /home/user/perf_logs exists.  
• The file /home/user/perf_logs/cpu_hog_metrics.log exists, is readable, and contains exactly three newline-terminated records in the precise TIMESTAMP|PID|CPU|RSS layout described above.  
• All three lines refer to the same PID.  
• The process is not running anymore.

Do not display the log file contents in your terminal output; simply ensure the file is created with the required data.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/cpu_hog.sh
-----------------------------------
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Very small infinite loop to eat CPU
while true; do :; done
-----------------------------------
It is executable (0755).

Verification procedure (for grader):
1. Confirm /home/user/perf_logs exists and is a directory.
2. Confirm /home/user/perf_logs/cpu_hog_metrics.log exists and is a regular file.
3. Read the file; insist on exactly 3 newline-separated lines, no extra blank lines.
   Regex for each line:
   ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}_[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\|[0-9]+\|[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?\|[0-9]+$
4. Capture PID from first line; all three lines must share identical PID.
5. ps -p <PID> should return non-zero (process not found).
